### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-09
I installed netbeans from the net beans website using this guide 
[http://connectwww.com/how-to-install-netbeans-ide-on-ubuntu-12-04/2286/](http://connectwww.com/how-to-install-netbeans-ide-on-ubuntu-12-04/2286/)

Everything installed fine , however when i click the shortcut in my menu nothing happens.

---

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-09
Try typing  the command 'netbeans' into a terminal. This should at least give us some feedback as to what's happening.

---

sudo apt-get install netbeans

If i run the installer again it says it is.

sudo apt-get install netbeans

?

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11

```
cat /usr/share/applications/netbeans.desktop
```

---

### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-11
cat: /usr/share/applications/netbeans.desktop: No such file or directory

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11
OK then, can you post the output of:
```
ls -l /usr/share/applications
ls -l ~/.local/share/applications
```

---

Net beans is there but it is in green?

-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root   273 Dec  9 18:37 netbeans-7.2.1.desktop

---

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11
That doesn't matter, we just need to see the contents so we can launch the program from a terminal and see any error messages. What's the output of:
```
grep Exec /usr/share/applications/netbeans-7.2.1.desktop
```

---

### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-11
Exec=/bin/sh "/usr/local/netbeans-7.2.1/bin/netbeans"

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11

```
/usr/local/netbeans-7.2.1/bin/netbeans
```

do i just run the above command?

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11
That's unfortunate, I was expecting an error message that would point to what the problem is.

Let me try installing it on my Xubuntu machine to see if I can replicate your issue.

Can you also post the output of:
```
java -version
```

---

### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-11
Ok, thanks I followed the guide i linked exactly

---

### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-11
java version "1.7.0_09"
OpenJDK Runtime Environment (IcedTea7 2.3.3) (7u9-2.3.3-0ubuntu1~12.10.1)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (build 23.2-b09, mixed mode)

---

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11
Try the following commands to install Oracle Java, some applications don't work properly with OpenJDK.
```
sudo add-apt-repository ppa:webupd8team/java
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install oracle-java7-installer
```

My installation of NetBeans worked fine just now, this is the only difference in our systems that I can think of. Also the tutorial you followed linked to a page telling you to install Oracle JDK.

---

### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-11
how would i go about wiping it from the system to try again?

---

### Post by Cheesemill on 2012-12-11
The commands I posted above will install Oracle Java alongside OpenJDK but set Oracle Java as the default, no need to uninstall OpenJDK.

Once you've done this try launching NetBeans, it may just work.

If not then you will need to reinstall NetBeans. To remove it use:
```
sudo /usr/local/netbeans-7.2.1/uninstall.sh
```

Then reinstall using the method you followed first time round.

During installation the installer has a drop down box that lets you chooses which version of Java to use, make sure Oracle Java is selected.

---

### Post by MWhitt on 2012-12-11
That worked thanks , it was the wrong JDK i had installed.

---
